sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the updated state:
- White diamond is at ~(40, 33) - this is where the purple square WAS
- There's a NEW purple square at ~(49, 30) with magenta center at ~(49, 30)
- Dark gray square is at ~(59, 27) with purple pixel inside
- The path now goes: white diamond → light gray path → purple square → light gray path → dark gray square
- The purple ring is still at the old position (12 p pixels)
So the pattern is:
1. White diamond moves to clicked location
2. A new purple square appears further along the path
3. The path is redrawn
Wait, let me reconsider. Looking at the adjacency:
- 17(W) is at ~(40,33) - the white diamond
- 21(p) is the purple square at ~(49,30) with magenta child 23
- 27(G) is the dark gray square at ~(59,27) with purple child 28
- Light gray path: 19(w) at ~(43,32), 20(w) at ~(45,31) connect to purple square 21
- Then 25(w) at ~(52,29), 26(w) at ~(55,28) connect purple square to dark gray square
So the path is: white diamond → w pixels → purple square → w pixels → dark gray square
The next waypoint to click is the purple square at ~(49, 30). Let me click on its center (the magenta pixel at ~(49, 30)).
Wait, but the efficiency budget says "2 of about 22 target actions so far." That's a LOT of actions. Maybe I'm not understanding the game correctly. Let me think again...
Actually, maybe the game has many levels and each level needs ~22 actions. Or maybe there are many waypoints. Let me just continue clicking through the path and see what happens.
Let me click on the purple square at ~(49, 30).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
Recent findings: White diamond moved to ~(40,33). A new purple square appeared at ~(49,30) with magenta center. Path: white diamond → w → purple square → w → dark gray square at ~(59,27).
World model: Clicking waypoints moves the white diamond there and spawns the next waypoint. The sequence continues along the diagonal path.
Plan: Click the purple square at ~(49,30) to advance.
